memory_tier.summary: Restart memory after the bounded Keycloak observe-only boundary packet.

memory_validity.summary: High-confidence restart memory because this slice captured a direct live Keycloak boundary with service, route, and journal evidence.

summary: A bounded Keycloak boundary packet is now recorded for the live condition on 10.167.2.150: keycloak.service is failed, 8443 is absent, /algosec-ui/login still returns 200, the Keycloak OIDC path returns 503, and Metro still answers isAlive true.
highest_risk_observation: The slice proves the support boundary cleanly, but it does not yet prove the underlying root cause behind the Quarkus EOF startup failure.
recommended_next_action: Reuse this packet as the imported-module drilldown pilot proof and only go deeper into Keycloak startup inputs if the next support question truly needs a narrower module-internal classifier.
resolved_block_summary: Transport and separation questions are no longer the uncertainty for this slice; the remaining uncertainty is Keycloak-internal startup cause.
handoff_summary: Target preflight and heartbeat both passed before evidence capture.
The current live Keycloak-down condition is reproduced as a module boundary, not as a top-level UI edge failure.
This slice is evidence-bearing and small enough to judge the imported-module drilldown pilot positively.
The active parent task remains deepen_dependency_aware_playbooks.
